extra.reasoning:
The property requires that setting dizziness=true (all else unchanged) never lowers acuity. dizziness appears in only one rule: r8 [priority 100] IF (leg_swelling and slurred_speech and sweating and dyspnea and confusion and pallor and fever and chest_pain and focal_weakness and back_pain and rash and dizziness) THEN acuity := self_care. r8 sets acuity to the LOWEST value (self_care) and has the highest priority (100), so when it fires it dominates all other matching rules.

Counterexample: take the input where the 12 flags listed in r8 are all true except dizziness is false (severe_headache is irrelevant; set false).
- BEFORE (dizziness=false): r8 does not fire. Rules r1(pallor), r2(slurred_speech), r7(chest_pain), r9(back_pain), r13(dyspnea) all fire at priority 3 setting acuity:=emergent. Various priority-2 rules also fire but lose on priority. Highest-priority match is priority 3 -> acuity = emergent (the highest acuity).
- AFTER (dizziness=true): all 12 of r8's conjuncts are now true, so r8 fires with priority 100, beating every priority-3 rule -> acuity = self_care (the lowest acuity).

Setting dizziness true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, which is strictly lower. This violates monotonicity. The witness_assignment gives the 'after' state (dizziness=true) that yields self_care, whereas the same input with dizziness=false yields emergent.
